categories.name: AlexKontorovich/PrimeNumberTheoremAnd
categories.reasoning: This project aims to formalize specific, well-known mathematical theorems (Prime Number Theorem, Prime Number Theorem in Arithmetic Progressions, and potentially Chebotarev density theorem) rather than building general-purpose infrastructure or teaching materials.

github_metadata.description: blueprint for prime number theorem and more

categories.name: FormalizedFormalLogic/Foundation
categories.reasoning: This repository is dedicated to formalizing mathematical logic in Lean 4, with specific focus on propositional logic, completeness theorems, and Kripke semantics. It represents a structured formalization effort of a specific mathematical domain (formal logic) rather than general-purpose tooling or pedagogical materials.

categories.name: ImperialCollegeLondon/FLT
categories.reasoning: This repository is dedicated to formalizing the proof of a specific, famous mathematical theorem - Fermat's Last Theorem. It represents a focused effort to formalize a particular mathematical result rather than building general-purpose infrastructure or teaching materials.

github_metadata.description: Ongoing Lean formalisation of the proof of Fermat's Last Theorem

categories.name: YaelDillies/LeanCamCombi
categories.reasoning: This repository explicitly aims to formalize specific university courses (Cambridge Part II and Part III) in combinatorics. The focus on course material and lecture content makes it primarily a teaching and learning resource rather than a standalone mathematical formalization or library.

github_metadata.description: Formalisation of the Cambridge Part II and Part III courses Graph Theory, Combinatorics, Extremal and Probabilistic Combinatorics in Lean

categories.name: dwrensha/compfiles
categories.reasoning: This repository is a collection of olympiad-style math competition problems formalized in Lean 4, with a dashboard tracking their status. It serves as a catalog of formalized math problems, which aligns with benchmark repositories that focus on math competition problems and problem sets.

github_metadata.description: Catalog Of Math Problems Formalized In Lean

categories.name: fpvandoorn/carleson
categories.reasoning: This repository is explicitly dedicated to formalizing a specific mathematical theorem (Carleson's theorem) in Lean, complete with a blueprint and dependency graph. It focuses on proving one particular significant result rather than building general-purpose infrastructure or teaching materials.

github_metadata.description: A formalized proof of Carleson's theorem in Lean

categories.name: leanprover-community/aesop
categories.reasoning: Aesop is a general-purpose automation tactic for Lean 4 that provides reusable proof search infrastructure. It's designed to be extensible and used across many different projects, similar to how `simp` or other core tactics are used, making it fundamental tooling rather than a specific formalization or pedagogical resource.

categories.name: leanprover-community/batteries
categories.reasoning: Batteries is explicitly described as an extended library providing reusable data structures and tactics for general use in both computer science and mathematics applications. It serves as foundational infrastructure similar to mathlib4, designed to be a dependency for other Lean 4 projects.

github_metadata.description: The "batteries included" extended library for the Lean programming language and theorem prover

categories.name: leanprover-community/duper
categories.reasoning: Duper is an automatic theorem prover tool that can be added to existing Lean 4 projects to provide automated proof capabilities. It serves as reusable infrastructure similar to Isabelle's Metis, making it a general-purpose library for the Lean ecosystem.

categories.name: leanprover-community/quote4
categories.reasoning: This is a reusable metaprogramming library that provides type-safe expression quotation functionality for Lean 4. It's general-purpose infrastructure designed to be used by other projects that need to construct Lean expressions in meta-level code.

github_metadata.description: Intuitive, type-safe expression quotations for Lean 4.
